overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. Overall, the account has a good following and demonstrates consistent engagement across platforms. LinkedIn has the largest follower base, while Bluesky shows strong engagement rates on recent posts.

contentThemes.description: Focus on research related to computational methods, social media analysis, disinformation, and information operations.
Posts related to hiring postdoctoral researchers and PhD students.
Announcements about new publications, conference presentations, and software releases.

recommendations: Diversify content formats, including images and videos, to capture more attention and improve engagement.
Engage directly with followers by responding to comments and asking questions to foster discussions.
Tailor content to each platform's audience to maximize relevance and engagement. For example, keep LinkedIn posts more professional and formal, while Bluesky can be more casual and conversational.
Utilize relevant hashtags to increase discoverability on each platform.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky demonstrates high repost rates suggesting content resonates well with the audience. Focus on continuing to share research findings and engage with the community.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a smaller, yet engaged audience. Focus on sharing research and participating in relevant discussions within the AOIR.social instance.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base and good engagement rates on job postings and grant announcements. Continue posting professional updates and research opportunities.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
